title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
pattern: Graph â€” Multi-source DFS/BFS from Boundaries (Reverse Flow)
coreIdea: Find cells from which water can flow to both the Pacific and Atlantic oceans by reversing the problem: run DFS/BFS from all Pacific-adjacent border cells and separately from all Atlantic-adjacent border cells (flowing 'uphill' since water flows to lower/equal height), then intersect the two reachable sets.
recognitionCues: grid problem with two 'target' boundaries (opposite edges)
flow/reachability condition based on height comparison between cells
reversing the search direction from boundaries inward is the key insight
approach: Run multi-source DFS/BFS starting from all cells adjacent to the Pacific (top and left borders), moving to a neighbor only if its height >= current height (reverse flow), marking reachable cells. Repeat separately for Atlantic (bottom and right borders). The answer is all cells marked reachable in BOTH searches.
